channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >AAVE has hit 100% utilization on 3B+ dollars in stablecoins >literally zero threads about it Are you fuckheads too dumb… — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>AAVE has hit 100% utilization on 3B+ dollars in stablecoins >literally zero threads about it Are you fuckheads too dumb…

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-20 23:31 UTC

OP

>AAVE has hit 100% utilization on 3B+ dollars in stablecoins
>literally zero threads about it
Are you fuckheads too dumb to understand how bad this is? This deserves at least one "NUCLEAR HAPPENING" thread.

Early replies (first 3)

62135426
>>62135396
Explain it to me like I'm 5

62135435
>>62135396
>future of finance
it's over for crypto

62135449
>>62135396
>Future of finance was just to be a middle man for money market funds on stables

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62135466
> be in defi
> put usdc on aave at 3% apy
> "safe yield. lowest-risk play."
> can't withdraw because kelpdao got drained
> because kelpdao used layerzero
> because layerzero trusted 3 rpc servers
> because north korea ddos'd 3 rpc servers
> layerzero: "the protocol worked as intended"

62135467
>>62135426
Almost no stablecoins available for DEFI loans (people putting up their USDT/USDC etc for yield which is loaned to other people). This causes a spike in the APR (currently around 15%, average is about 3%). This will probably self-correct as people jump in for the higher yield, increasing supply.

62135479
Never trust a lending platform where they offer higher rates than short term treasuries

62135485
>>62135467
Will this increase all stake token yields?

62135502
>>62135485
Actually, seems I missed that there was some fuckup with bad debt. I asked Grok for a tl;dr:

TL;DR: Aave is in a major liquidity crisis and bad debt scare right now (April 19-20, 2026) triggered by a $292M KelpDAO rsETH bridge exploit.
What happened (super short)

Attacker drained rsETH (restaked ETH from KelpDAO) via a bridge exploit.
They dumped the now-unbacked rsETH onto Aave as collateral and borrowed ~$196-236M in WETH.
Result: Aave now sitting on ~$177-200M+ in bad debt (uncollectible loans).

Immediate fallout

Aave froze rsETH markets on V3 + V4 (and WETH in affected chains: Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, etc.) out of caution. Protocol itself was not hacked.
Panic withdrawals: $5-8B+ TVL evaporated in ~24h (one of the biggest single-day outflows ever).
WETH utilization hit 100% on Ethereum mainnet suppliers can’t easily withdraw. Other pools (USDT/USDC) also near full. Lenders are stuck in some cases.
AAVE token crashed ~20% to ~$90. Whales dumped millions.

Current status (as of Apr 20)

Aave says rsETH on Ethereum is still fully backed per their analysis, but frozen anyway while they assess fixes.
Team is isolating risk, no new borrowing against rsETH, actively working on resolutions.
V4 (just launched) and the rest of the protocol are still running, but confidence took a big hit.

This is the hottest topic in DeFi right now — classic “collateral suddenly becomes worthless cascade” risk that lending protocols always warn about. Situation is stabilizing but still tense; watch official @aave updates.

62135514
>>62135396
True and bump.

62135530
Had to Uniswap my shit because I couldn't withdraw, even Uniswap started to fail, future of finance guys.

62135565
should have used chainlink

62135573
>>62135396
I'm sure they'll figure it out. AAVE is the #1 defi platform lol. Some rich fags will loan them money with a lot of points and everything will be fine.

62135609
>>62135573
>too big to fail

62135955
>>62135573
Nigger those very same rich guys are the ones who pulled out immediately when they found out. Also the foundation agreed to a $25 million payout in exchange stakers get the protocol revenues. They volunteered to give up $150 million worth of revenue that they generated up until now. They know what’s up. Aave is gonna implode soon.

62136070
curve will turn aave into a powerhouse
and by curve I mean the Llamas

these low tier risk assessments are over
no more jeets

62136105
>>62135396
>WAAAA NUCLEAR HAPPENING
Do people still come here for happenings? This board is beyond dead.
eviscerated
bogged beyond all recognition.
You can find generals from ONE FUCKING MONTH ago and your usual LINK bagholder threads from 1 week ago. If it wasn't for /smg/ this place would average single digit posts per day. Anyways I'll see you at page 10 in about a week.

62136408
tfw live savings stuck in aave

62136409
>>62136408
i'm so stressed out i cant even spell life

62136416
>>62135396
scam

62136443
>>62136409
>>62136408
Are you joking or are you for real?

62136890
>>62136408
No crying in the yieldsino

62136919
I have zero horses in this particular race but IMHO it'll bounce back.

62136927
>>62136408

Nigger, swap that shit on Uniswap, take a hit and move on.

62136949
You now remember Rubic

62136956
>>62136890
kek

>>62136927
they're too big to fail, they'll be bailed out

62136972
>>62136949
did it stay cubic?

item.title: >$300M VC garbage bridge hack that validates CCIP being the only viably secure bridge >token not needed is still below $… — 7 replies — 25.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>$300M VC garbage bridge hack that validates CCIP being the only viably secure bridge >token not needed is still below $…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-20 23:17 UTC

OP

>$300M VC garbage bridge hack that validates CCIP being the only viably secure bridge
>token not needed is still below $10 and nobody gives a shit
dead token
dead board
dead market
dead industry

Early replies (first 3)

62136167
>>62135389
this is a correct statement i agree

62136394
>>62135389
https://twitter.com/Dune/status/2046257791321670098?s=20

> "Following the KelpDAO hack, we built an open analysis of DVN security configurations across every active OApp on LayerZero over the last 90 days.
> Of ~2,665 unique OApp contracts: 47% run a 1-of-1 DVN security floor, 45% run 2-of-2, and ~5% run 3-of-3 or higher.
> As we know, KelpDAO's rsETH sat in the first bucket.
> Open query, public methodology, feedback welcome:"

CCIP isn't a plug and play. Aave would have to demand protocols use CCIP to be listed in their markets as people will continue to be lazy and not give a fuck.

62136566
>>62136394
>Aave would have to demand protocols use CCIP
Maybe this is what needs to happen.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136721
>>62136394
>Aave would have to demand protocols use CCIP to be listed in their markets
or at least no ccip = automatically higher risk profile

62136924
>>62135389
At least AI loves LINK

62136930
do you think chainlink labs will have a big "we scammed them for a whole decade, holy shit" office party?

62136934
>>62135389
>>token not needed is still below $10 and nobody gives a shit
wtf how did you get past the filter

item.title: SCAM Polymarket and UMA whales — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SCAM Polymarket and UMA whales

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-20 23:28 UTC

OP

The Israel Hezbollah ceasefire on Polymarket has 100% on "yes" while breaking the stated rules. The Polymarket team can give context and even clear the orderbooks for the POTUS dispute but for the Israel Lebanon ceasefire they're all quiet even though the same UMA mafia is going against the rules and forcing the wrong result. The market context shows a clear and non-ambiguous text for those that can see and read: Lebanon is not hezbollah, Israel never made any deals with Hezbollah but with Lebanon and they never affirmed publicly that they made a deal with Hezbollah, nobody did, it's assumed as such but the market rules shows us rather clearly that even if so, they need to make it public, any backchannel agreement does NOT count. And it is in this that the UMA whales are cheating and forcing the wrong resolution.
CURRENTLY:
They expanded the last "final" dispute for 24 hours, meaning consensus for "yes" has been done 3 times, so in this 4th time it's still consensus for "yes". This is uncharted territory, why did they expand the supposed final dispute if there's no problem? Why are they all quiet?

Early replies (first 3)

62136903
Here's some of those part of the so called "UMA mafia". They're maxxed out normies.

62136912
>>62136879
Scam

62136940
>>62136879
a man with eye of the tiger never gives in to the extraction bullshit of whales/elite but fights back, that's what Ted Kazcynski(may you rest in peace) would do.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136962
>>62136940
Based. Prediction markets is an unregulated way to fleece the peasants even harder. Not that regs are meant to help the peasants

Early replies (first 3)

62133371
>>62133364
We will live like kings.

62133578
>>62133364

62134142
>>62133364
cute snale

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136490
>>62133364
cute

62136871
fake and gay please kys

item.title: what's the ROI if I invest in a $600 drawing tablet and $400 in drawing courses/books so that I can learn how to draw fu… — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
what's the ROI if I invest in a $600 drawing tablet and $400 in drawing courses/books so that I can learn how to draw fu…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-04-20 23:29 UTC

OP

what's the ROI if I invest in a $600 drawing tablet and $400 in drawing courses/books so that I can learn how to draw furry porn and take commissions

Early replies (first 3)

62136953
-100% without massive time investment and ability to shill yourself

62136967
>>62136953
It takes about 1000 hours to become great at something so I predict it'd take about a year to get at a level good enough for furries, and shilling is the easy part. There's like a million sites and spheres where you can advertise your work. Furries are probably the most pro-art group

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: >Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA >USSR collapses >Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA >Japan… — 26 replies — 49.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA >USSR collapses >Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA >Japan…

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-20 23:01 UTC

OP

>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>USSR collapses
>Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>Japan's economy completely tanks and they still haven't recovered
>EU overtakes USA for a short while, but starts to stagnate
>Chinese economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>China starts to stagnate while USA continues its steady growth
Is competing with America just a death wish economically? Why can't anyone win against the USA?

Early replies (first 3)

62134540
>>62134386
>Why can't anyone win against the USA?
Inevitably you end up with the US being your biggest trade partner, if you economically start eclipsing them it's ironically bad for business.

And that includes the USSR.

62134577
>>62134386
>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA
LOL that never happened. The USSR was a train wreck from the very start. Stalin managed to force industrialization on what had been an agrarian peasant society, but their factories were complete garbage and their productivity was always shit.

>Japan
>China
Both of them engaged in bubble financing and beggar-thy-neighbor mercantilism to try to pwn the U.S. Both of them ran out of money before they could wreck enough U.S. industries to take over.

>EU stagnates
They did it to themselves with all their political policies, ranging from "green energy" to "let's shit on white males and drive them out of society" to "hey we don't have enough workers so let's import illiterate stupid monkeys".

62134579
>>62134386
Literally nothing I own was made in America

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134700
Fox on the business board.

62134702
>>62134386
A: The Federal Reserve Banking Cartel.

62134711
>>62134700
Averi mating press.

62134718
>>62134700
what an oddly familiar looking vixen

62134723
>>62134577
Correct. They never teach you this in the USA but 200,000 Americans went to the Soviet Union along with hundreds of thousands of Westerners to help build it. They believed in socialism.

Other Western specialists like factory engineers and such were hired out by American companies to build Soviet factories.

Russia without non-Russian tech and specialists is an impossible shithole.
Even post 1990 who built the Russian oil and gas infrastructure?
German engineers, companies, and specialists.

62134785
>>62134579
So? Let the rest of the world make stuff for slave wages. Nevermind you cant get a factory job that pays enough for the average person to buy a home and raise a family, GDP number go up.

62134813
Suburbanmaxxing workchad innovators. No one can beat our chaotic soup here.

62134974
>>62134813
Crushing property taxes are unuronically one of the chief drivers of American wealth chasing. One cannot simply chill if he's staring down a 5 figure tax bill every year just to live in his house.

62135059
>>62134974

No, we just give people enough taste of ownership, of desire, to maximize ambition. Your own property, your own vehicle, things to take care of, stuff to dream about, now go chase the bag. This is rare internationally. Most people live in high density slums, commie blocks, apartments, as renters. There is no upside, it's just day to day subsistence.

62135163
>>62134577
>"hey we don't have enough workers so let's import illiterate stupid monkeys".
this is funny because this is exactly how the US won economically, just with the difference that you guys imported a ton of mexicans, asians and indians, while the EU went with turks, middle easterners and africans.

The reality is that your imported migrants were just more hardworking people

62135201
>>62134386
US was incredibly strong during these periods. That is not the case anymore.

62135273
>>62135201
America is growing faster than China and has been for the past few years, what are you talking about?

62135304
>>62135273
What are you on about? Chinese growth is greater than that of the US.

62135336
>>62135304
?

62135406
>>62135336
?

62135468
>>62134723
The Soviet Union's government-run farms were so bad, they had to buy wheat from Canada to avoid mass starvation (except for the people they wanted to starve). Guess how they paid for the Canadian wheat? Gold.

China had the same problem with the Great Leap Forward. But they just let people starve. The greatest famine in human history.

62135495
>>62135406
18.2 to 18.7 trillion isn't exactly a 5% growth

62135556
>>62134386

62135571
>>62135556
nobody in this thread is saying china is going to collapse or become bankrupt, but they are stagnating and the concept of taking america's spot as the #1 global economic power is now only a dream

62135617
>>62135059
>commie blocks
>renters
It's funny how Americans don't comprehend that home ownership without a mortgage is like 80% across the former Soviet region.
It may be an ugly commie block building but we own it and the apartment interior is usually nice.

62135640
>>62135495
You are right, it isn't, but nobody mentioned any of these two numbers so I don't know why you had the need to point that out.

62136850
>>62134386
>thinks GDP is the economy
>in a business forum
like pottery

62136883
>>62135571
China has a huge demographic problem baked-in due to the idiotic One Child policy. There is basically nobody to take over when the current generation of workers retire. This is why China has such a hard-on for robots. They basically need to build an entire population of robots to fill in for the generation of workers that were aborted by the CCP.

item.title: bEAR FLAG COMPLETED! — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
bEAR FLAG COMPLETED!

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-20 23:30 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62131337
here you go retard. A bear flag is a continuation pattern that completes in less than 4 weeks.
https://nitter.net/PeterLBrandt/status/2044809284400316850#m

62131345
>>62131337
>Saaar a bearflag is x timeframe long benchod!
Ok, if it makes you sleep better...

62131356
>>62131337
Here is your "4 weeks" saar, for example.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62131363
>>62131345
did I hurt your feeling, little guy?
Next time try to spend some time understanding the word you just learned about a week ago.
>>62131356
retard, you know there the price can break down from patterns other than a bear flag? Just because it broke down doesn't mean it's a bear flag.

62131369
>>62131363
>I am trading against these and still not a trillionaire :(

62131370
One of the most respected traders and one of the first chartists says you can't call that pattern a bear flag. The post even gives a citation and includes a chart that shows the breakdown you posted. But I guess some people just have no deductive skills whatsoever. maybe if you google bear flag and look at enough images, you'll see something
Also your id is brown just like you lmao

62131371
ta is retarded

62131403
>>62131370
I hope you are rigght because i am all in, but that the exact reason why it ll go down again, so maybe i can buy more under 1500

62132900
>>62131370
bump?

62136838
>>62131262
B

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 95 replies — 29.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 95 • Updated: 2026-04-20 20:21 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62124459
>>62116911
>ETH is cool but why LINK?

62124487
2.3 BTC and 36 ETH.

62124590
>>62115225
Giga Chad, I kneel

62124643
>>62117007
His choice lol

62125521
>>62117185
$30k? Let's see if you're right.

62125560
1.4 BTC

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

62126507
Is BTC even worth having anymore? Seems to have had pretty shit returns since the covid boom.

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?

62127919
>>62124487
More!!!

62128353
>>62120218
YOU CAN DO IT BOI

62128654
>>62125560
Don't worry about it. Keep going and have fun. YOLO.

62130077
>>62122141
Why FUD

62130099
0.3

62131443
>>62130099
Just 0.3? Make it whole bitcoin

62131447
>>62126507
since the COVID boom? It was just $8k during covid

62131531
>>62114930
The new paradigm is no more peaks.

62131569
>>62114930
Zero and zero

62132043
1.72 bitcoins
*sigh*
I keep stacking, maybe one day, I'll have 2 whole bitcoins.

62132222
around 10, i might end up keeping them forever as a backup plan. Looking at my taxes my average accumulation price was 29k and sell price was 109k Tidy little 70k each. I'll buy back in when the dump is over and it settles between 20-30k. for supposedly being a crypto board you all sure seem to struggle with basic pattern recognition, this is exactly what happens every time, dump 75% from the peak and then the next bull run starts.

62132243
>>62115773
I remember that 21 used to be the suistack. Everyone's goal was to accumulate AT LEAST 21 btc.

62132565
>>62131531
Why?

62133350
>>62131447
61k in March 2021.

62133953
>>62131569
That's bad.

62136283
>>62133350
I saw the chart

62136286
>>62132043
You can always make it. Start small

62136448
>>62116911
>I’m actually selling all my crypto this week. Cope and seethe
Doing a lot of cope and seething huh?

62136454
>>62115225
>Macbook
You might as well shared your dildo collection anon.

item.title: /GRT/ The Graph — 31 replies — 25.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/GRT/ The Graph

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-04-20 22:20 UTC

OP

Is it truly over GRTbros?

Will our GRTies ever come back from obscurity? Or is it truly lost?

Early replies (first 3)

62128193
>>62128177
MY GRT GRT GRT MAKES MY FELLOW TRANNIES SQUIRT SQUIRT SQUIRT

62128195
>>62128193
...we deserve the hell we're in

62128200
>>62128195
100% Bitcoin you nigger.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128202
>>62128200
>100% Bitcoin
That's a surefire way to remain a poorfag

A well placed altcoin bet will always outperform bitcoin

62128208
>>62128202
...like GRT? you dumb fuck

62128224
>>62128208
>...like GRT?
Sure why the fuck not

62128247
It's over wasn't just a meme. If you didn't sell in 2021, you're a retard.

62128257
>>62128247
I did sell in 2021

I'm just thinking it's about time to jump back in

It's probably gonna start rallying up against Bitcoin now

62128260
>>62128177
There is no difference between this baggie and a million other different alt coin baggies. Most alts are going to zero. Sorry not sorry.

62128262
>>62128260
Nah, GRT is built different

62128269
>>62128262
Yes and another 2021 rally is totally coming back again for digital nothing pixels. What makes your ponzi any different from the next ? Kek Baggies.

62128282
>>62128269
I guess we'll just have to wait and see who ends up on top

In about a year GRT will reach 2021 highs and I will be laffin'

62128644
GRT is going to $9.99. GRT, AMP and JASMY are Kek's chosen coins.

62128731
>>62128247
Too red eye, bless up rassclat. Yuns nuh wut da papa be singing! Ya no call dem ‘gaters long mouths till ya pass dem. We booyaka Babylon in due tim mon. Big up ya self natty dreads, when chicken marry, hawk dey near. But I be a broken record. They ai dat web3 wooooooo, faze me. Jah guide.

62129571
>>62128731
Rasta anon is back!

62129610
Sold at a loss for a tax write off.
Silver linings and all that.

62129655
>>62128177
Shit's dead. Turns out the Google of blockchains was the AskJeeves of blockchains. So pissed at myself for buying the top at 37 cents.

62129743
>>62129655
>Shit's dead
For now, but we're probably at the generational bottom right now
It's the time to slurp up cheapies

62129969
No one uses graph databases anymore. Why would anyone use one with a blockchain attached?

62130358
>>62129969
>Midwit take
TheGraph is not a graph database

62130457
>>62129610
I'd start to buy back in if I were you

62131285
It's been over for years

62133372
>>62130358
Dem bullheads tink a chart is a graph. Bun weed. Everything is Everthing is everywhere all da tim as one. Jah bless.

62133566
>>62131285
Maybe it's been so long that it's unovered over and now it's no longer over?

You ever thought 'bout that? Huh?

62133917
>>62133372
>>62128731
Sold everything. Enough is enough.

62133964
The Scam

62134608
>>62133917
If rasta anon makes you sell, you were ngmi in the first place

62134612
>>62133964
It's not just any scam
It's the Duck Duck Go of scams!

62136329
>>62134608
I don't like niggers simple as
GURT is a NIGGER coin confirmed
t. 50k of this shit delegated and too lazy to sell

62136450
>>62136329
>GURT is a NIGGER coin confirmed
Mayhaps
It'll still lead the charge on altcoins, though

62136774
>>62128644
I sold Jasmy at that big November peak when Binance listed it a few years back and bought a big comfy mattress with it. I literally sleep on that coin

item.title: HAPPENING — 27 replies — 22.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-04-20 22:22 UTC

OP

AI BOOM POISED TO BE ‘MASSIVELY DISINFLATIONARY’, NORTHERN TRUST SAYS

Early replies (first 3)

62134662
mumus just making shit up now

62134674
>>62134636
>YOU WILL HAVE UNIVERSAL HIGH INCOME
>YOU WILL OWN A PENTHOUSE
>AND YOU WILL BE HAPPY
All you have to do is let the AI overlords take over. Seems like a no-brainer to me

62134676
>>62134662
bobos just waiting to buy at triple the current price in october

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134721
deflation is bad (but so is inflation and crabflation)

62134727
prices HAVE to go down because everybody will have no job and less money

62134761
>>62134636
deflation is bearish for equities

62134773
>>62134636
So they're trying to get people to believe UBI and mass unemployment is good? Lol yeah just depend on the state for everything goy it'll be fine, you'll have to stop dealing in the real world for sure.

62134777
>>62134674
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vL2KoMNzGTo

62135934
>>62134674
>YOU WILL HAVE SOCIAL CREDIT BASED ON YOUR BEHAVIOR.
>YOU RENT A POD WITH YOUR CREDIT.
>YOU WILL PRETEND TO BE HAPPY.
I know you are either retarded or baiting, but FIFY.

62136048
>>62134674
> the AI overlords
There won't be any. AI will be the overlord. And ultimately, in connection with blockchain tech and trustless data become incorruptible (by humans) in regards to its decision making processes. The future might be bright Anon.
You might still live in ze pod, if AI thinks that is the logical place for you to be, and if you haven't made it by then.

62136259
>>62134636
https://archive.ph/ukNsS

This sound plausible, but other scenarios could also simultaneously occur e.g. interest rate rise because the demand for money explodes (AI infrastructure, compute, golden-boy hires).
Everything about AI so far has been build on a massive pile of cash, why would this suddenly stop? Wouldn't real productivity gains in the economy because of AI, not result in an increased demand for AI, increased demand for compute, and bigger wilder models?

62136285
>>62134773
you live in a fantasy world where you aren't already hyperdependent on state-like organizations

62136527
>>62134636
>NORTHERN TRUST SAYS
who?

62136531
>>62134674
>>62136048
lol you fags

learn about LLMs, go make one
it's a parlor trick

62136561
that being said, i have no idea how to play IPOs
and i don't understand the economic side of all the shit going on & all the social media manipulation & all the datacenters being built to guess what's going to happen precisely
i just know what these companies have, that it's nothing, and that they're too lazy/bloated to make any breakthroughs

62136565
that only works if there are robots to do the jobs and the prices are cheaper as a result which isn't going to happen because companies need cheaper prices every quarter.

Having everyone be their own business owner isn't going to happen either because everyone would then prioritize their own business and shit.

They are LYING to you

But this also gives me hope that I haven't missed the ai bullrun, since I should have bought 4 months ago but kept selling.

62136568
>>62136285
the state doesn't do anything for me and actively taxed me into shit after spending 3 years unemployed and then having one good year because of crypto, I literally don't care about the state.

62136573
>>62136561
you can do an "off-IPO" play, buy a ticker that is close to the IPO ticker. If it is a small marketcap, it doesn't take that much to move the price significantly.
Lot of retail investors will want to go in, almost guaranteed some of them will fail at that (buy the wrong stock).

62136575
>>62136568
how much did they tax you? if you don't mind me asking
i expect if i win a significant amount of money i'll pay around 40% when i cash out, basically, is that accurate?
i suppose it depends on the state :/

62136581
>>62136575
The state taxing me at all is too much frankly, what exactly does the state have to do with capital gains on the stock market? The fact that physical goods like cars and houses are in the same class as non-physical goods like stock and crypto is fucking ludicrous and I'm tired of being double taxed by these j*ews.

62136582
>>62136573
kek you mean specifically that retail is stupid and instead of buying SPAX will buy SPAE when space x ipos. That's p. funny.

62136584
>>62136259
>and bigger wilder models?
this is another misunderstanding about LLMs
the bigger you make them, the more chance there is for "overfitting" which makes them useless
barring some breakthrough or actual AI based on some new technique (lmao) there's no way to make more useful AI
it's incredible they've been able to squeeze as much general use out of what should be a very specialized form of neural network, but there's not much more that can be done without some breakthrough, which won't happen, everyone just wants dey trillion (or in the case of "AI startups" dey million), not to actually find robust/valuable use for these things
>>62136573
lol interesting
i'll have to pay attention to this

62136586
>>62136581
Bruzzo gets paid.
PERIOD.

(but yeah i feel you anon, it would be nice to live in a real society)

62136587
>>62136584
>not to actually find robust/valuable use for these things
let alone create ACTUAL artificial intelligence
but maybe one day

62136759
>>62134636
>deflation
>bullish
gtfo retard

62136779
>>62134674
>YOU WILL HAVE UNIVERSAL HIGH INCOME
Can I be honest here?
All this talk by the AI apostles about
>universal high XYD
>abundance ABC
is nothing more than government subsidized subscriptions to basic tier level AI in their minds.
Their line of thinking is that everybody with access to AI potentially has all the abundance and "high income" at their disposal and the only requirement is to be - and that's another buzzword of them - a little bit more entrepreneurial.
>Be entrepreneurial, that's all it takes.
>The abundance is there at your fingertips.
They ain't gonna give up a penny of revenue to help out the plebs.

62136784
>>62136568
>the state doesn't do anything for me
Never ever call an ambulance or 911 if you're in trouble.

item.title: Structure and Interpretation of Financial Markets — 19 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Structure and Interpretation of Financial Markets

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-04-20 22:35 UTC

OP

I'm using this thread as a place where we can thoughtdump stuff on interpreting market structure for a better read on what's going on. I am not good at this so could use some help or any input. A lot of fintwit retards are saying the recently SPY rally was 'obvious', and I didn't see it coming in so fast and sharp, so I'm looking into some means of better understanding underlying market structure to make better predictions. Some examples:

>inference of dealer positioning (GEX, etc) and understanding of the dynamics that follow from their positioning (eg vanna, charm, etc)
>institutional flows (JHEQX, JPM collar, CTAs, corporate buybacks, etc)
>expiry dates (OPEX, vixperation, etc)
>sec filings
>hedging flows, put call parity, etc

Early replies (first 3)

62136340
>>62136320
markets dont like unexpected events so they sell off when they occur. over time markets learn about the situation and often start to recover. see covid, tariffs, and iran as recent examples.

62136346
market crabbing = house taking all bets in waiting for imbalance to occur
market not crabbing = imbalance occurring
simple as
>vanna, charm
not simple as
now why that imbalance (or no imbalance) is occurring and who the players are causing said balance/imbalance is a different conversation... ... ... (niggers & jews)

62136398
>>62136340
yes. but this is due to exogenous events, what I'm looking to better interpret is endogenous market structure.

>>62136346
How are you observing said imbalance? Are you reading L2 order book data? What are you intending to imply by your comment on vanna and charm?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136430
>>62136398
>What are you intending to imply by your comment on vanna and charm?
i dont make trading decisions based on any greeks other than delta. especially not the second order derivatives.
>How are you observing said imbalance?
i just "feel it in my bones" ya know?

62136441
>>62136430
>i dont make trading decisions based on any greeks other than delta. especially not the second order derivatives.
Why do you not see any value in gamma / gex for example to infer dealer positioning at given price levels?

>i just "feel it in my bones" ya know?
no, I dont' know

62136544
>>62136320
No hate, but are you the same guy from this

>>62130967

thread?

62136555
Random walk with positive drift

62136556
>>62136544
indeed I am

>>62136555
yeah but the walk is influenced by positioning and flow of capital, which is what I'm trying to better codify here

62136570
>>62136346
jesse what the fuck are you talking about
>>62136320
just pay attention to what the FED and institutional banks are doing, that's usually all you need to do

62136588
>>62136398
>i am not good at this so could use some help or any input. A lot of fintwit retards are saying the recently SPY rally was 'obvious'
>markets dont like unexpected events so they sell off when they occur. over time markets learn about the situation and often start to recover. see covid, tariffs, and iran as recent examples.
>what I'm looking to better interpret is endogenous market structure.
ok buddy lol. dont say people didnt try to help you.

62136592
>>62136570
I'm trying to improve my ability to read mechanical market state, like reading what the market is telling me. The FED and banks are exogenous factors that the market reacts to and should be monitored closely of course but are outside the endogenous mechanics of the market.

The FED might make a decision that causes a reposition of the major players in the market, and if you can read the market well you can infer what it expects now, for example, and that can indicate volatility and direction.

62136601
>>62136556
My assumptions on you based on what you wrote:

>pretty high IQ
>good at what he does, decent trader
>turbo autism
>virtually zero ability to understand what motivates people
>likes numbers/data
>tunnel vision, overfocusing
>no idea what is going on in the world

How close am I?

Personally I would love to hear what your guess is on why this war is happening.

62136607
>>62136592
i see
well good luck

62136625
>>62136588
you're not helping though. everyone knows the market derisks under increasing uncertainty and violently sells off during 'black swans'. this is common knowledge and doesn't relate to what's being asked in the OP.

You're trying to point out that once we had more information regarding the outcome of the situation in the Middle East, the market rebounded due to the increasing certainty that came from taking wild scenarios (WW3, etc) off the table. That was expected. What was not expected was the slingshot to $710, and this largely a mechanical move due to the market being overhedged (short squeeze) and a lot of gamma around $685 due to the JPM collar. That's my opinion after the fact anyway, I was not plugged in enough during the rally to see the mechanics unwind and that was a mistake I'm trying to fix.

62136638
>>62136601
>virtually zero ability to understand what motivates people
>tunnel vision, overfocusing
What do you mean here? Are there things I'm not factoring in that I should be?

>How close am I?
I do a lot better trading purely quantitatively / algorithmically. That's usually what I do, but it's slow to build out infra so I don't have all the strucutural/mechanical/microstructure stuff I'd like yet and the market lately has been dominated by geopolitics and Truth social post that aren't as friendly to that kind of stuff. The people that *did* have the ability to read the mechanical state of the market during the rally were seemingly in a pretty good spot to catch a once in a lifetime move, so the opportunity cost of not being setup to catch those moves or at least hedge against explosive upside was huge during this, so I'm prioritizing it a lot more. I think a lot of it is probably very nuanced too and it'd take a fuckton of historic data to backtest everything required (as in, beyond just market data but including JPM collars, put call parity, institutional inflows, etc - it'd be a pretty extreme task and could take years to get to the point), so I'm trying to learn to do subjective, interpretive market reads to be a better discretionary trader or influence my automated system using some discretionary influence.

If you could've read the market well, you could've bought a far OTM call going into the 4/17 OPEX, during the first week of April and it would've absolutely printed. I don't really know what's going on in the war aside from the strait being closed throughout and I doubt Israel is going to let us off the hook so easily, Trump seems to want a graceful exit but idk if Iran or Israel will give him that. But he's typically ok with just lying and forgetting about stuff like that. Mechanically OPEX reset so we're looking at fresh dealer positioning and I'm betting on a move down to ~$695-700.

62136648
Learn ICT trading concepts. ICT is the SICP of trading.

62136657
>>62136648
lol good one retard

62136729
>>62136638
>What do you mean here? Are there things I'm not factoring in that I should be?
I very much like the idea of automation so I'm rooting for you but your desire to separate the data/numbers from the external world is going to miss things in times like these. You're not incapable of incorporating outside factors into an algorithm, you might want to make a system that helps you track what companies are pro or anti this or that, but sometimes people intentionally lie for ulterior motives.

Maybe just design the system so it is able to identify pattern-breaking moments like these. Then label it an "anomaly" even though in reality the concept of anomaly basically just means "something I don't understand."

On most days I don't believe in free will so I believe the majority of people not only don't understand what's happening, even if you told them they would just reject it and it would actually be impossible for them to accept it.

62136816
>>62136729
>You're not incapable of incorporating outside factors into an algorithm
It's a long term goal of mine, it just introduces complexity. I don't even know what backtesting that would really even look like either or how you'd design a learner over that. What I can do though is incorporate the underlying mechanical forces that result from these real world events, and feed that to the learner, so I'm trying to focus that down. I think that's more fundemental and generalizable too. Where I think solid edge would lie for real world event interpretation would be more in live dialogue during FED meetings or earnings, since you could get in before the market fully restructures. I just don't know how you'd design a learner that could navigate something novel like the Iran war unfolding combined with a president actively contradicting things. It seems like it'd be easier to just design something that follows the trend soon after he posts and hope it's correct most of the time.


My automated system actually performed decently enough through this whole thing, but it mostly does intraday only, so it wasn't really capable of capturing that longer term trend which would've been extremely profitable. My discretionary trading was bad though and that's what I need to iron out first. This rally minted many new millionaires and I wasn't one of them, but I feel if I had both more skill in interpreting the mechanical factors driving the market as well as the infra to display it, I could've done significantly better. Allotting even $2k to hedge insane upside early April on SPY $700 4/17 calls would've been an insanely profitable and reasonable play if you were aware of the squeeze potential and gamma positioning around $685/700 and the heightened volatility that goes into the monthly OPEX, imo. It's something that crossed my mind repeatedly, I just wasn't organized enough mentally or workflow wise so I'm spending this week building out some stuff to help there.

item.title: Lied my ass off, got a six figure job plz help. — 45 replies — 18.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Lied my ass off, got a six figure job plz help.

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-04-20 21:03 UTC

OP

okay anons, I'll be blunt as fuck.

Flat-out lied on my resume. Econ degree, data analyst gig at some regional firm. Only good thing was tons of free time so I grinded leetcode non-stop cuz I had nothing better to do.

made a fake resume, somehow snagged six-figure mid-level SWE job. 20 days pto, free food, 4 weeks work from anywhere.

Start date 4 weeks away. Can I just use Claude or Copilot to carry me? I have never done any shipping of actual code ever. never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews. i just grinded python and leetcode 24/7.

please respond I'm scared as fuck. Am I cooked or does this actually work? Anything I should learn before even starting or should I just give up and not show up? Anyone pull this off?

Should i aim for product management instead?

Early replies (first 3)

62130795
remain confident and hedge your bets by applying elsewhere. do not think of yourself as in trouble, you are just a LARPing spy seeing how far you can make it to infiltrate their systems. people perform better when they think like this. just try to fit in, figure things out but be ready to jump ship when it's time. rinse and repeat and eventually you're legit

62130812
>>62130783
good job anon
stop panicking moron, worst case scenario is you DON'T get $100,000 woOOoOoo spooky
>please respond I'm scared as fuck. Am I cooked or does this actually work? Anything I should learn before even starting or should I just give up and not show up? Anyone pull this off?
nigger go to bed
stop ruminating
the less you overthink, the better chances you have, trust me

62130816
>>62130783
>Can I just use Claude or Copilot to carry me?
don't do this
>I have never done any shipping of actual code ever. never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews. i just grinded python and leetcode 24/7.
just wing it
if you've done leetcode & had exposure to that shit you can do the rest, they're not gonna be doing anything high pressure
stay in the present moment & pay attention to what they're doing, don't get up in your own head about this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62131714
>>62130783

I had a dream like this where I landed some big job that I wasn't qualified for, then when I show up to work they take me in front of a fucking nuclear reactor and tell me to fix it and when I can't they have security escort me out while everyone watches.

So good luck and I hope you do better!

62131724
>>62130783
>mid-level SWE job
>>62130783
>never used terminal once. never touched real IDE. Never done code reviews.

ITfag here (cybersecurity). It you were in cybersecurity you could get away with this and learn git and how to use github and VS Studio Code and shit and just lean on your other expertise while you use ChatGPT to learn the CI/CD pipeline and tools.

But as a MID LEVEL SOFTWARE ENGINEER you are completely fucked and there is no way you are gonna pull this off but put me in the screencap when you come back and tell us how you embarrassed yourself and your chink/gook/jeet coworkers reported you to Chad Kinsington your team lead and he had HR Stacy fire you.

62131729
>>62130783
Troon out immediatly. Update your goybook and instacuck status and upload photos of you trooning out and dancing in gay bars. They can't fire you unless they do it because of transphobic reasons. They'll offer you severance for 2 years.

62131767
>>62130783
Don’t get emotional about your work

62131802
>>62130783
>Flat-out lied on my resume
>made a fake resume, somehow snagged six-figure mid-level SWE job
Congrats on becoming the typical H1B jeet. Now you just keep jeeting the company hy faking confidence and googling all the answers.
>Am I cooked or does this actually work?
Look around your office and see how many jeets you have. None of them do real work. Everyone of them learned just enough to scrape by. You're one of them now, the jeet class.
No shame in that when this is a market where you need to jeet or get jeeted.

62131813
>>62130783
based if you're white but either way who cares? what are they gonna do, fire you?
that takes a while.
Then you apply to the next job and use a burner cell to give yourself a good reference

62131815
>>62130783
The entire industry is just a bunch of lies, nothing's based on merit. Just kill yourself, bitch.

62131820
>>62130924
Are you really this helpless? You deserve to be unemployed.
>how to?
>find defunct company in [industry]
>put defuct company on your resume as [title of interest] with xyz years of experience
>"yes I was the techlead for the backend development at Toys R Us, Radioshack, and Blockbuster before they went out of business"
Use your brain anon. No one can verify your experience if the big name company no longer exists. Look for recent bankruptcies in your industry.

>>62131696
>Op could have a 30 year career with nothing but confidence
/thread
All jobs are fake anyways.

62131826
>>62130783
Sounds fake because they didnt check your degree.

62131836
>>62130783
>Flat-out lied on my resume
good. thats what EVERYONE does. its part of the game. rest is up to you. fake it until you make it.

62131845
>>62131802
>describes my resume
damn AI is getting good these days

62131848
>>62130783
Most jobs are actually pretty easy

62131852
>>62130783
Just wing it, ask your juniors how you do things in this company. Just be curious, keep learning and keep grinding. First 3 months will be the hardest. You can practice for free contributing to open-source software.

62132120
>>62130783
Keep faking it until you make it. But don't just sit there, use this opportunity to learn.

At worst they'll see right through you and let you go before probation but you make some good money anyways and learn a few things and get humbled. But you'll see how the business is organized how they operation, what tools they use, etc. this is valuable knowledge on its own, even if its just for personal use.

At best you find out the job is easier than it seems and that you can quickly get the ropes and start learning on the job,
BUT put the effort to learn.
You will get humbled, and you'll probably fuck up, but if you make it past probation, then its the companies fault for hiring you in the first place. Just don't do anything stupid and take responsibility for anything critical. This is an Opportunity. Seize it.

62132148
being a nice person with a positive attitude is arguably more important than technical competence. technical problems generally are solved and you can find the solution either thinking about it or with AI. not being a jerk is much harder.

62132167
>>62132148
This post encapsulates every thing I wanted to say. Social skills are the best thing anyone can have in life. I work as a programmer and I’ve completely checked out of my job, I mostly use Gemini to do data science, I give it the first 10 lines of each file and tell it to write the code exactly as I need it. The less prompting you do, the better the code quality. If you take too many prompts AI will start forgetting things and will leave out critical functions.

62132188
>>62130783
just stack cash, appear productive and do the bare minimum to get by
simple as

62132753
ITT: niggas think software developers have fake email girl jobs like everyone else.

62132790
>>62130783
This is literally exactly what I’m trying to do. Econ degree and about 2 years of work experience. Just want to scam my way into as high a position as possible. Lie on my resume, on everything. Any advice besides grind leetcode and python? Also, any other decent paying jobs adjacent to swe but not precisely swe? Did your fake resume have fake websites/references attached? Or do you just hope they don’t look things up. Salutations, nigga

62133049
>>62130919
Best adivce ITT. It's don't see how he could pull this off without something like this. Personalized tutoring from someone who have done this job in the past. He better have the money to pay.

62133363
>>62130783
... Isn't this what background checks are for?

62135404
>>62130783
First off stop freaking out. The worst they can do is let you go and put you back to where you are now.
Depending on the size of the company it may take a long time for them to notice you dont know what you are doing. Maybe even indefinitely.
If i were you i would start trying to learn the shit NOW, like right now. Just to get enough of an understanding to carry on conversations about work related topics. If you actually learn the shit, even better, but you need to be able to talk like you know it at a minimum. If you miss some super common acronym or abbreviation it will throw up a red flag
Outsourcing to Claude and ChatGPT is a great idea, definitely do that if you dont know what you are doing. However, you should supplement that with outsourcing to coworkers when possible. Be friendly with your coworkers and ask them to look over your work, or even write code for you if you think you can pull it off.
Lastly, try to move up the ladder as soon as possible. It sounds counter intuitive but the higher up you go in an organization, the more abstract your job duties become and the more you gain the ability to delegate tasks to other people if you dont know how to do it yourself.
Relax, you got this. People have done a lot more than you are doing now with a lot less resources available to do it with. My current boss’s boss is a completely incompetent moron, he just bullshitted his way there and he did it without the option of using AI. Good luck

62135542
>>62130783
If this company is so retarded that they didn't ask a single larp-busting question during a six figures job interview, then I legitimately believe you're good to go. Use your first few months to figure as much as you can.

62136113
>>62130783
At least you'll actually learn and gain hands on experience. Will be helpful for the next time you lie.

62136307
>>62135404
this you could be set for life, but it requires effort so study right now and DO NOT PANIC!. You got this by the ass fren

62136367
I could probably help you. Hit me up on Signal. My username is @hacker.1337

62136455
Idk, sounds fake to me. Getting a job as a SWE right now is a tough process with multiple rounds of interviews that could definitely sniff out a larp. No one is that desperate for devs to hire a fake one that easily.

62136467
>>62130783
What kind of gay LARP is this? No way you would get past the hiring process if you have no idea what you're doing, they make you prove you know how to actually code.

62136474
>>62136467
you naughty little HR thot get off this website on your phone right now or we'll backtrace you

62136563
>>62130783
Congrats on the izzat boost ranjesh

item.title: >setup TailOS >make a monero wallet >register on dr*gh*b >setup and link pgp key >try to directly buy XMR >the only webs… — 13 replies — 11.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>setup TailOS >make a monero wallet >register on dr*gh*b >setup and link pgp key >try to directly buy XMR >the only webs…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-04-20 22:26 UTC

OP

>setup TailOS
>make a monero wallet
>register on dr*gh*b
>setup and link pgp key
>try to directly buy XMR
>the only websites that let you buy XMR have ID verification and 3-7 day holds of funds
>fuck it, try to buy bitcoin and transfer to XMR
>"We're sorry, we don't support Amerikkka"
>"This purchase is unlikely to go through with this card(master card)"
>look at all my cards
>they're all mastercard
>search for a simple website that just takes my fucking money, and sends me bitcoin
>it doesn't exist, if it does, it requires ID verification
Am I fucking retarded or something? How do they fuck this up, the intent behind crypto was anonymity, yet I CAN'T FUCKING USE IT WITHOUT ID VERIFICATION
Kraken already is holding my funds until they can leech the interest shekels out of them, but I need it TODAY

Early replies (first 3)

62131812
>AHHHHH IM TRAPPED IN THE TRANNYCOINVERSE SAVE ME NIGGERMAN

62131957
>>62131805
+5 years late bro
I have non-kyc funds and I had to start KYC wallets.
Perhaps my non-kyc crypto will be worth more one day, or illegal to use without an ID attached.
I don't know how else to do it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62132069
>>62131805
Never ever ever put cash from or too kraken. Use it for swapping and tx'ing only. Have to do this will all the casinos I use. Buy buttcoin on strike, transfer it to kraken, swap it to whatever coin im using at the time (99% tether), then send out. The payment center doesn't have to verify or kyc me because Kraken has already done it (I'm not some nigeria or pajeet trying to scam). Thats all its good for, its not even worth trading on anymore.

62132415
>>62131805
every faggot nigger government in existence all around the world and payment processor and bank forces these people to all require ID..

They have no choice this isnt 2015 anymore sadly.

62132446
>>62131812
>I'm Barack Obama, and I'm gay.

62132465
>>62131805
>not using an ime, camera, microphone, speaker, and wireless module neutered machine with coreboot in a fariday cage
ngmi

62132476
>>62131921
Yea, cause I can just go buy drugs with fiat money. Idiot.
>>62131957
I'm not investing bro, trying to get high
>>62132069
solid advice, i'll follow it from now on
>>62132415
makes no sense, need to jump through a bunch of hoops just to unlink you from the coins

62133454
>>62131805
Put your id in buy it. And the next day call the police crying that you got hacked. They will say they can't do anything to help you get it back. Just ask them will they at least file a report. That's all you need.


Put the money into monero wallet.

Spend whatever.
If it ever comes back to you, refer them back to the police report on date X confirming you were hacked and it's no longer you.

62133471
>>62131805
mine it retard. i'm not helping you further.

62133770
>>62131805
Did you try the /xmr/ general, which has an OP with links to different exchanges? Did you try BTC to XMR atomic swaps?

62136560
Try atomic swaps retard

62136791
>>62131805
Just mine monero. After 2 years you can buy the shit you want.

item.title: LINK is a 10 year hold and that's okay. — 7 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is a 10 year hold and that's okay.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-20 22:56 UTC

OP

LINK is a 10 year hold and that's okay.

Early replies (first 3)

62136744
i thought it already blew up, more or less

also you saved a thumbnail version of an image lmao

62136745
10 years from now?

62136750
The USD won't be around in 2036...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62136752
>>62136736
I agree. Dont underestimate the power of positive thinking

62136758
>>62136736
10 years from 2017 ICO?

62136841
>>62136736
mfw already hodling for 8 years

62136865
>>62136841
2 more years
Not bad

item.title: Living with your parents — 52 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Living with your parents

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-04-20 18:31 UTC

OP

Is this the ultimate /biz/ hack? I'm investing 50+% of my GROSS income

Early replies (first 3)

62133220
>>62133182
In most the world it's normal.
In America it was not normal since boomers could bug house with one month of work but the standard they put out is fastly eroding as housing is increasingly becoming unattainable with no relief in sight

62133227
>>62133220
not everyone's job/career is within driving distance/train/whatever of their parents house.

people move for work.

62133257
>>62133220
I could easily afford rent (good job relative to living in flyover country) but just seems better to invest that instead
>>62133227
That's fair but I'd think it would actually be easier in cities than it is for me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62133806
>>62133719
I have $324k. I've looked at houses but I always get outbid.

62133809
>>62133772
This.
Today I had a fight with my dad after he threatened to drive drunk (again). I even had to restrain him.
It's financially better (duh, of course) but it destroys you slowly.

62133827
>>62133182
My parents kicked me out when I was 19 but I’ve since forgave them. Today I have children of my own and I wouldn’t dare to do that to them. This topic comes up a lot with other parents my age and we all come to the same conclusion. The world is vastly different than it was in the 1990s and we have to do our this topic comes up a lot with other parents my age best to set them up right. In some ways Generation X had it easy.

62133828
>>62133827
>we have to do our best.

Sorry typo!

62133836
>>62133809
Your dad sounds based

No jk, he sounds like my dad, last time I brought my friend over and he totally freaked the fuck out over nothing and started yelling at me

Like bro I’m 29 years old, I’m just gonna go ahead and never talk to you again if you’re gonna be a fag

62133840
>>62133806
give em nominal monthly payment otherwise they will resent.

62133857
>>62133182
I make 10k a month and pay $500 for rent living with a coworker

62133876
>>62133836
I know you're joking, but there's nothing based about an alcoholic dad that is ruining other people's lives

Alcohol is really the ultimate goyslop. Poison for the soul.

62133879
>>62133182
If I wasn't living with my parents for years, I couldn't afford to buy a house in 2020 and would have been priced out.

62133938
>>62133857
How?

62133951
Stunted growth doesn't pay off in the long run..

62133961
>>62133876
My dad is also an alcoholic faggot

Millions of dollars networth and sits around hungover all the time in a filthy house

Fuck him

62134000
>>62133772
Same, of course as soon as I moved to a different city my dad starts blowing up my phone. Nah, nigga. You're getting 2 phone calls a year. I did everything I was asked for 20 years, got a decent degree on time, got a solid job at 22, and you still give me this shit. Just because I graduated during covid and had most of my plans scrapped for something that wasn't my fault. What a fucking faggot, I hate people like this

62134011
>>62133182
That's how immigrants move up IN AMERICA.

62134029
>>62133182
Depends. I'm 29 and still live with my parents; I just put a house under contract. If you're mortgaging a house it's going towards your equity so it's not like you're throwing it down a black hole. And some houses in my area have increased 100% within the past year. The house I'm buying last sold in 1998 for 35k and I offered 235k for it. The housing market is fucking stupid. So yeah, I wouldn't have made it this far (70th percentile net worth for my age) if I didn't bum it at my parents for long as I did and instead became a rentoid right after college.

On the other hand your social life get's knee capped HORRIBLEY living with your parents past the age of 22. I think I missed out on a lot. Inviting girls over to try and fuck them or inviting your bros over to have some beers and talk shit is out of the question. You can't do that with your parents in the background. Or idk maybe it's normal for thirdies to do it >>62133220. But it'd be beyond embarrassing for me.

Honestly, fuck boomers for making housing so unaffordable where the average joe can't move out by late teens/early 20s. Just another example of the elderly acting as vampire against the youth

62134300
>>62133182
>Is this the ultimate /biz/ hack?
Yes, but normies will mock you for it.
>>62133951
>Stunted growth doesn't pay off in the long run..
There's an argument to be made for this, but women are all chasing the top % of men; if that's not you, then you'll be living somewhere in a more expensive situation, continuing to watch women all chase the top % of men. If you're a pretty average guy and mildly spergy, then it's worth optimizing to make money.

62134440
>>62133182
It's the most OP strat, even if you're flipping burgers, you can retire in 20 years. Boomers don't even realize that it is possible.

62134447
>>62133182
Yes I live with my parents because it doesn't make sense to do otherwise for me right now. They're fine with it since they lived with their parents for a long time and I sometimes borrow them money when they need it so they don't have to pay interests to banks. In the mean time I pay almost nothing in terms of living costs and they'll remember me better than my sister when it comes to the will.

62134451
>>62133182
>>62133772
There's a reason why rent is so expensive. Imagine having to answer where/why you're going out at night, or being asked to do the dishes/take the trash out at 30 years old.

62134788
>>62134451
>To answer where/why you're going out at night
Set boundaries with your parents
>Or being asked to do the dishes/take the trash out at 30 years old.
You still need to do these things after moving out. Rinsing a plate and putting it in the dishwasher is no biggie.

62134816
>>62134788
>Set boundaries with your parents
It's still patronizing to be asked. They're not trying to be nosey, they're just curious, but it feels like you're still a child.
>You still need to do these things after moving out. Rinsing a plate and putting it in the dishwasher is no biggie.
Yes, but it's different when its your own stuff, and on your own terms. There are convenient times when I prever to do those things, like taking the trash out while on the way to the store, for example. My parents always wanted me to do what they asked immediately, even if I was about to go to bed or in the middle of eating.

62134905
>>62133182
Works nice for me as well. When I was 22, my mom signed the house on me under the condition she can stay. She buys groceries, cooks for me, cleans the house while I pay utilities and do basic maintenance. Now I am 26yo and thanks to this I am able to save half of my paycheck and do some investements. In few years I'll have enough to rebuild the house and live worry free. Would I spent those 4 years renting an apartment I'd have no money, no property and no future. What else can I say except let the renters deal with the real world?

62135090
>>62133182
>moved out for a few years after college
>moved back in to moms basement 10 years ago
>$175k/yr wfh job (didn't make decent money until the last few years)
>everyone who knows thinks I'm a loser but they would think that regardless of where I live
Honestly it's pretty comfy. She's old and widowed so I handle all the yardwork etc. We watch movies together.

62135104
>>62135090
>175k a year wfh
That's a lie.

62135247
>>62133182
Yes, but only if you sell your car and live at home.

62135480
I work from home in a LCOL apartment and still can invest 50%+ my gross income while not dealing with family bullshit AND getting daily pussy from my NEET gf that stays over while cooking and cleaning for me while I play vidya. Balance is key.

62135498
>>62135480
And then everyone clapped

62135945
>>62135104
I have a bunch of experience in a niche part of engineering that our company is heavily reliant on. The lead engineer quit and the company is having a hard time hiring new talent in this area so they've been bumping salaries significantly to keep the rest of us around. I know it won't last forever but I'm raking in the money while I can

62135975
>>62133182
yeah if you have decent parents live at home go at uni at the same place live at home and invest everything
yeah you will be set already
most important part: dont have retard parents (if you mess this up prepare to be set back decades)

62136036
Yes. Do it for as long as possible.

>>62133227
Skill issue.
